series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the status of accounts in which the agency owes money to firms or individuals; includes invoices, receipts or bills of sale, check registers, and subsidiary registers; also includes stop payment notices

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning amounts paid to individuals as allowances or reimbursements for travel or other business expenses

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning receivables owed and collected; includes billing statements, records of payment received, remittances, subsidiary registers, overpayment or refund records, deposits, fines and fees assessed, and collection of past due accounts; also includes records concerning accounts sent to NC Debt Setoff Program for collection

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records of accounts deemed uncollectable; includes returned checks, write-off authorizations, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Determined uncollectable Agency Policy: ______________ PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: statements created by the State Treasurer indicating monthly activity for each disbursing account

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 11 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the collection by the State Treasurer of forged state warrants

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: source documents received by the State Treasurer for Federal Reserve Bank transactions, including warrants (State checks) and deposit transactions

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: includes forms authorizing electronic transfer of monies via wire transfer or automated clearing house (ACH); includes direct deposit forms and ACH bank reports
records concerning rates and fees of agency services and assessments; also includes rates for fines and penalties SEE ALSO: (above) Accounts Receivable

annual financial reports or other reports generated to inform decision-making SEE ALSO: Official Publications (PUBLIC RELATIONS), Reports Written by the Agency (AGENCY MANAGEMENT) (continued on following page)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: monthly or quarterly reports generated for operational purposes SEE ALSO: Official Publications (PUBLIC RELATIONS), Reports Written by the Agency (AGENCY MANAGEMENT) (continued on following page)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: year-end summaries of receipts and disbursements not in the North Carolina Financial System (NCFS) or legacy North Carolina Accounting System (NCAS); includes profit and loss statements

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: daily, monthly, and quarterly details of receipts, collections, and disbursements not in NCFS

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: general and detail records of transactions in NCFS or legacy North Carolina Accounting System (NCAS) maintained by the Office of the State Controller

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 6 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: bank statements, canceled checks, deposit slips, and reconciliation reports

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: financial records regarding agency travel; includes documentation of advance payments and reimbursements

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: requests and authorizations for travel; includes forms and itineraries

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: budget requests and appropriations; includes justifications and requests for revisions

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years Then: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: administrative budget records; includes research, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: budget certifications prepared and approved by the Office of State Budget and Management (OSBM)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 35 years Ω THEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 2 biennia. Records will be held for agency 31 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: periodic reports (weekly, monthly, quarterly) detailing actual year-to-date revenue and expenditures

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: annual June 30th report from the Office of State Budget and Management (OSBM) detailing actual fiscal-year-end revenue and expenditures

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 3 years (and conclusion of all audits) for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: budget allotments issued by OSBM

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 13 years Ω THEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 3 years. Records will be held for agency 10 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: budget revisions issued by OSBM

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 7 years Ω THEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 2 years. Records will be held for agency 5 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: directives regarding the state budget issued by any governing body that are not captured in the official minutes SEE ALSO: Archival Correspondence (AGENCY MANAGEMENT) for directives regarding the state budget issued by the director of the Office of State Budget and Management

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: forms used by the Office of the State Controller to enter data into the North Carolina Financial System (NCFS) or legacy North Carolina Accounting System (NCAS)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records relating to grant applications that are denied by the agency and to unsuccessful grant applications submitted by the agency; includes applications, evaluations, and correspondence

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Grants Administered by Agency (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: records relating to state pass-through grants administered by the agency; includes applications, evaluations, grant monitoring, modifications and amendment request, progress reports, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Submission of final report PLUS: 5 years ΩTHEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer records to the State Records Center 2 years after grant is closed. Records will be held for agency 3 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_title: Grants Administered by Agency (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: records relating to Federal pass-through grants administered by the agency; includes applications, evaluations, grant monitoring, modifications and amendment request, progress reports, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Submission of final report PLUS: 3 years ΩTHEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer records to the State Records Center 1 year after project is closed if no litigation, claim, audit, or other official action involving the records has been initiated. If official action has been initiated, transfer to State Records Center after completion of action and resolution of issues involved. Paper records will be held for agency 3 additional years after official notification of program year closeout from the United States Department of Housing and Urban Development and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: records relating to disbursement of funds from the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS); also includes financial records, rate sheets, invoices for payment, utilization data, and other related records
series_metadata.legal_citation: Retention 42 CFR 422.504(d) DHHS Office of the Controller memorandum (April 20, 2016)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Inactive or submission of final report PLUS: 10 years ΩTHEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center 2 years after designated inactive. Records will be held for agency 8 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: records relating to grants issued by the agency using state appropriations or other agency funds; includes applications, evaluations, grant monitoring, modifications and amendment requests, progress reports, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Submission of final report PLUS: 5 years ΩTHEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer records to the State Records Center 2 years after submission of final report. Records will be held for agency 3 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_description: final reports and deliverables or other documentation of the distribution of funds

tracking_data.comments: For awards subject to a recapture period, transfer final reports and deliverables to the State Records Center 3 years after conclusion of the recapture period. For all other awards, transfer final reports and deliverables to the State Records Center 3 years after conclusion of agreement period. Records will transfer immediately to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records relating to the agency’s oversight of grant funding; includes fiscal and allocation reports, audit reports, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Submission of final report PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Grants Received by Agency (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: records relating to major grants received by the agency that begin new initiatives or promote core functions of the agency

tracking_data.comments: For awards subject to a recapture period, transfer final reports and deliverables to the State Records Center 3 years after conclusion of the recapture period. For all other awards, transfer final reports and deliverables to the State Records Center 3 years after conclusion of agreement period. Records will transfer immediately to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_title: Grants Received by Agency (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: records relating to routine state or non-governmental grants received by the agency; includes applications, notifications, periodic reports, time and effort (cost-sharing) reports, modification requests, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Submission of final report PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning designation of depository banks, savings and loan associations, or trust companies and related maintenance of the accounts
records concerning special funds maintained by the Department of the State Treasurer; includes disbursements and receipts

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 15 years for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the sale of bonds or certificates of debt issued by the State Treasurer. Includes bond transcripts, numbers, date of issue, when and where payable, at what premium, and to whom they may have been sold or issued

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center 5 years after date of completion for immediate transfer to the custody of the Archives.

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the sale of bonds or certificates of debt issued by state departments, institutions, agencies, or commissions as authorized by the Governor and Council of State. Includes bond transcripts, numbers, date of issue, when and where payable, at what premium, and to whom they may have been sold or issued
transaction schedules for projecting revenue on investments to be funded or collected

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year end PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: performance investment reports issued by broker or investment firm and other related performance review records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy* Agency Policy: _______________

series_metadata.series_description: records documenting the awarding of renewable scholarships; includes applications, award letters, conditions and stipulations, agreements and contracts, disbursement statements, progress reports, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Fiscal year close PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records relating to decisions regarding loan and scholarship applications; includes evaluations, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Notification of applicant PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning loans granted by the agency; includes documentation of intent to proceed, loan agreements, promissory notes, letters of credit, statements, notices of principal and interest due, monitoring of wages, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Satisfaction of obligation PLUS: 7 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: loan/application register

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Notification of applicant PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning applications that are denied by the agency or awards that are declined by the recipient

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Notification of applicant PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Solicitations (including Invitation for Bids [IFB], Request for Proposal [RFP], and Request for Quote [RFQ])
series_metadata.series_description: records documenting accepted bids, proposals, and quotes to supply the agency with goods or services. Includes advertisements, tabulations, awards letters, good faith effort documentation, notices, evaluations, and related documents
series_metadata.legal_citation: Confidentiality G.S. § 132-1.10 G.S. § 136-28.5 01 NCAC 05B .0103 09 NCAC 06B .0103 Retention 01 NCAC 05B .1901 09 NCAC 06B .1401

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination or expiration of procurement contract PLUS: 5 years ΩTHEN: Destroy*

tracking_data.comments: Transfer to the State Records Center after 3 years. Records will be held for agency 2 additional years and then destroyed.

series_metadata.series_title: Solicitations (including Invitation for Bids [IFB], Request for Proposal [RFP], and Request for Quote [RFQ])
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning rejected bids and proposals to provide the agency with goods or services

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Rejected PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: approved requests, authorizations, and other documents concerning purchased goods or services

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination or expiration of procurement contract PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: denied requests, authorizations, and other documents concerning purchased goods or services
series_metadata.series_description: RETAIN UNTIL: Rejected PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Qualified Products Lists (QPL) and Approved Products Lists (APL)
series_metadata.series_description: records identifying products approved for purchase under State contract NOTE: As of 2018 the Department of Administration no longer maintains QPLs

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: informal requests used to gather information from potential suppliers of goods or services

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reference value ends ± THEN: Destroy* Agency Policy: _______________

series_metadata.series_description: records identifying vendors approved to conduct business with state agencies; also includes list of vendors debarred from doing business with State agencies SEE ALSO: Historically Underutilized Businesses (HUB) Records (ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT)
records concerning the assessment of tax value or appraisal of assets SEE ALSO: (above) Fee Schedules
records concerning the levying of garnishments by the Department of Revenue; includes notices, correspondence, and other related records SEE ALSO: Garnishments (HUMAN RESOURCES)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination of deduction PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: public listings of delinquent taxpayers by the Department of Revenue SEE ALSO: Garnishments (HUMAN RESOURCES)
remaining records held by the Department of Revenue related to delinquent taxpayers SEE ALSO: Garnishments (HUMAN RESOURCES)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Paid PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the seizure and sale of property by the Department of Revenue

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Paid PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the allocation of tax credits; includes applications, evaluations, correspondence, and other related records SEE ALSO: Employer Workforce Records, Eye Disability Reports (PUBLIC ASSISTANCE AND SUPPORT SERVICES)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Expiration PLUS: 6 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: tax information returns generated by the agency (e.g., 1098, 1099, W-2, W-3) to be reported to the Internal Revenue Service and furnished to the other party to the transaction
series_metadata.legal_citation: Confidentiality G.S. § 132-1.10 Retention 04 NCAC 24D .0501(a) IRS Publication 15

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Submitted to taxpayer and/or IRS PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: certified tax records provided by the Department of Revenue; includes correspondence, completed powers of attorney and declarations of representative forms, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*
